text.title: AWS Three-Tier Architecture (VPC, ALB, App, Database) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: us-east-1a AZ failure → full outage; SLO blown.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimised dev/staging single-AZ and copied to prod.
antipattern.fix: Add second AZ to all tiers; rebalance subnets. NAT GW per AZ.

antipattern.symptom: Subnet refactor changes CIDR; SG rules silently allow nothing; service breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote rules with hardcoded CIDR ranges.
antipattern.fix: Convert all inter-tier rules to SG-by-ID references.

antipattern.symptom: DB subnet has IGW / NAT route 'just in case'; credential compromise → direct data exfil.
antipattern.root-cause: Author lifted-and-shifted from a single-subnet VPC.
antipattern.fix: Remove all internet routes from DB subnet. DB SG accepts only from app SG.

antipattern.symptom: Cost optimisation merged single-NAT-GW config to prod; AZ failure → app cannot reach external APIs; outage.
antipattern.fix: Set single_nat_gateway = (var.environment != 'prod'). Validate per-AZ NAT GW count.
